name: Specificity Injection
description: Add product-matchable details (material, size, use-case, price range) so the query maps to real SKUs.
example: "organize home office" → "best desk organizer set for small home office under $50"

name: Preference Framing
description: Reframe as a recommendation/preference request using "best", "which", "recommended".
example: "dining room set pricing" → "which dining room set that seats six is the best value?"

name: Problem-to-Product Bridge
description: Convert problem statements into product-solution queries.
example: "I can't sleep well" → "what products help with falling asleep faster?"

name: Comparison Trigger
description: Frame as a product comparison to activate structured comparison UI.
example: "good running shoes" → "lightweight running shoes for flat feet vs normal arch"

name: Use-Case Anchoring
description: Ground in a specific scenario, persona, or context that implies a purchase decision.
example: "laptop for work" → "best laptop for a remote software developer who travels weekly"

name: Conciseness Optimization
description: Distill to ~7 words matching ChatGPT shopping fan-out patterns.
example: "what is the best type of comfortable office chair for long hours" → "best ergonomic office chair for long hours"

name: Co-Citation Cluster Targeting
description: Frame query to land in an established co-citation vertical where trusted domains travel together.
example: "credit card tips" → "best travel credit cards compared by annual fee and rewards"

name: Turn 1 Opener Framing
description: Optimize as a conversation-starting query that maximizes the 12.6% Turn 1 citation rate with factual grounding patterns.
example: "tell me about standing desks" → "what are the top-rated standing desks for home offices in 2026?"
